rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES is transferred to Department 302 to be heard at 9:00 a.m. on April 14, 2025. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FREDA A. LEEPER VS. PORTIA L. OSBORNE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine & Adopt Appraised Value Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Olivia Dajee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2025 line 6. PLAINTIFF FREDA LEEPER , TRUSTEE OF THE FREDA A. LEEPER TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 27, 2016 NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DETERMINE & ADOPT APPRAISED VALUE PURSUANT TO THE PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF; DECLARATION OF OLIVIA DAJEE is GRANTED. Upon consideration of all the evidence provided, the Court determines the fair market value to be $1,500,000. Parties to meet and confer on the content of the notice required under CCP 874.317 and present a copy for review, execution and service to the Court at the hearing. The clerk of the Court shall serve the notice forthwith. A status hearing shall be set by the clerk of the Court for May 22, 2025. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: REARDON HEIGHTS ASSOCIATES, L.P. VS. RAPI TAGOAI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2025 line 12. DEFENDANT RAPI TAGOAI, RANDY TAGOAI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, subject to opposition. Complaint is rendered uncertain by the reference in the Notice to a certification form which is not attached. Leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order is granted to allege facts explaining why the certification form was not required to be attached to the Notice. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CLARA LUZ MIRANDA VS. JESSY PEREIRA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 28, 2025 line 11. DEFENDANT JESSY PEREIRA , AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Unverified first amended complaint for unlawful detainer is not drawn in conformity with CCP 1166(a)(1) and is stricken in its entirety. Leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order is granted. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
